differential_diagnoses: Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (JC virus granule cell neuronopathy)
next_steps: Perform CSF PCR for JC virus (specifically testing for granule cell neuronopathy), VZV, HHV-6, and arboviruses including Powassan virus
Perform repeat CSF cytology with immunocytochemistry and potentially brain biopsy if CSF studies are nondiagnostic
Perform CSF paraneoplastic antibody panel (including anti-Yo, anti-DNER, anti-Hu, and anti-Ma2)
Continue supportive care and reassess immunosuppressive regimen
Consider brain biopsy for definitive histopathologic diagnosis if non-invasive testing remains negative

differential_diagnoses: Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (JC virus granule cell neuronopathy)
next_steps: Perform CSF EBV EBER-in situ hybridization and PCR on cytologic specimens
Perform CSF PCR for JC virus, VZV, HHV-6, and arboviruses
Perform CSF TCR gene rearrangement and repeat cytology with immunohistochemistry
Perform MRI of the entire spine with and without gadolinium contrast to evaluate for leptomeningeal enhancement
Consider brain biopsy of the cerebellum or meninges if non-invasive workup is negative and clinical decline persists
